title: MOSS: Self-Evolution through Source-Level Rewriting in Autonomous Agent Systems

llm_extracted.research_problem: Autonomous agentic systems are static after deployment and cannot fix structural failures that exist in code rather than text-mutable artifacts.
llm_extracted.main_contribution: MOSS enables source-level self-rewriting for autonomous agent systems, allowing them to fix structural failures unreachable through text-layer modifications.
key_findings: MOSS lifts four-task mean grader score from 0.25 to 0.61 in a single cycle without human intervention
Source-level adaptation is Turing-complete and a strict superset of every text-mutable scope
Source-level adaptation takes effect deterministically rather than through base-model compliance and does not erode under long-context drift
sustainability_or_social_impact: reduces need for human intervention in production failure fixes
evidence_sentences: Autonomous agentic systems are largely static after deployment: they do not learn from user interactions, and recurring failures persist until the next human-driven update ships a fix.
We present MOSS, a system that performs self-rewriting at the source level on production agentic substrates.
On OpenClaw, MOSS lifts a four-task mean grader score from 0.25 to 0.61 in a single cycle without human intervention.
